raw_consumer_statement: I ordered and paid for a dress on XXXX for a special event on XX/XX/XXXX. It was required and guaranteed to be received by XX/XX/XXXX. I never received the dress. I was offered a replacement dress in XXXX that was past the guaranteed date and event date, which did not satisfy the terms of the order. XXXX never gave me a coupon code or discount code for the replacement dress. XXXX gave me a fake offer. I filed a dispute with AMX on XXXX. The shipper was XXXX XXXX, which I have never heard of. They don't offer insurance or claims for shipping miss-delivery, loss, theft, or damage. AMX will not refund my money. Shipping delivery is not a guarantee that items are in a box or that the customer received a perfect box. Shipping delivery confirmation is only a guarantee that the shipping XXXX label landed at a location, which can be the wrong location. It does not guarantee condition, the specific location, or the contents. Damaged boxes and contents are not confirmed delivery, but wrong, tampered with, and damaged delivery. The government and the police need to step in and fix the fraud that happens in the shipping industry with delivery confirmations. I want AMX to refund me {$150.00} for a dress that I never received but paid for. The other option is to give me a real coupon code, discount code, or gift card for {$150.00}.
